title: Advancing institutional-grade real estate investment in the UAE
excerpt: In the ever-evolving world of real estate, few names in the UAE command the level of trust and expertise that institutional-grade investment demands.
content:
In the ever-evolving world of real estate, few names in the UAE command the level of trust and expertise that institutional-grade investment demands. As the market matures, investors are increasingly seeking platforms that combine rigorous due diligence with transparent governance structures.

The UAE’s real estate sector has undergone a transformation, moving from speculative trading to a more sophisticated, institution-driven market. This shift has created opportunities for firms like BlackOak to bridge the gap between private capital and institutional-quality assets.

With disciplined asset management and clear reporting standards, the future of real estate investment in the UAE is being shaped by those who prioritize quality, transparency, and long-term value creation.
author: BlackOak Advisory

title: Imran Sheikh Recommended in Spear's 500 Property Index 2026
excerpt: We are proud to announce that Imran Sheikh has been recommended among the leading property investment, finance, and management services advisers working with high-net-worth clients.
content:
We are proud to announce that Imran Sheikh has been recommended among the leading property investment, finance, and management services advisers working with high-net-worth clients, in the Spear’s 500 Property Index 2026 — an annual Spear’s Magazine #Spears500 listing of top professionals in luxury real estate.

At BlackOak, we are honoured by this recognition, which reflects our commitment to excellence across real estate investment, fund management, and bespoke advisory for HNWIs in Dubai and the wider GCC. Grateful for the partnerships that continue to drive our four verticals: brokerage, advisory, development, and funds.
author: BlackOak Communications

title: Golden Visa: How Property Investment Secures Residency
excerpt: A comprehensive guide to how property investment in Dubai can qualify you for the Golden Visa, offering long-term renewable residency for investors.
content:
The UAE Golden Visa programme has become one of the most attractive residency-by-investment schemes globally, and Dubai’s property market sits at its centre. Investors who purchase property worth AED 2 million or more can qualify for a 10-year renewable residency visa.

The Golden Visa offers significant benefits including the ability to live, work, and study in the UAE without the need for a national sponsor. Recent changes have made the programme even more accessible, with off-plan property purchases now qualifying under certain conditions.
author: BlackOak Advisory

title: Lack of plots gives Palm Jumeirah added air of exclusivity
excerpt: The price of property on Dubai's Palm Jumeirah is only going to increase as the limited supply of available plots drives demand ever higher.
content:
The price of property on Dubai’s Palm Jumeirah is only going to increase as the limited supply of available plots drives demand ever higher. As one of the world’s most iconic man-made islands, the Palm has always commanded a premium, but recent market dynamics have pushed values to new heights.

With virtually all land plots now developed and no expansion plans on the horizon, the scarcity factor has become the Palm’s greatest asset. Investors and homebuyers alike recognise that owning a piece of the Palm means holding a finite, irreplaceable asset.

This exclusivity, combined with world-class amenities and a lifestyle that is difficult to replicate anywhere else, ensures that Palm Jumeirah will remain at the pinnacle of Dubai’s luxury property market.
author: BlackOak Research

title: Real Estate Legends
excerpt: We explore the multifaceted importance of real estate in the UAE, encompassing economic, social, and cultural dimensions.
content:
We explore the multifaceted importance of real estate in the UAE, encompassing economic, social, and cultural dimensions that have made this sector a cornerstone of national development.

From the visionary leadership that transformed desert landscapes into world-class urban environments, to the entrepreneurs and developers who brought these visions to life, the UAE’s real estate story is one of ambition, innovation, and resilience.

These legends of real estate have not only shaped the physical landscape of the UAE but have also created a legacy of excellence that continues to attract global attention and investment.
author: BlackOak Communications

title: What is unique about Dubai Marina Apartments for Sale
excerpt: Dubai Marina, a famous beachfront location in this energetic metropolis, offers its inhabitants a variety of opulent living experiences.
content:
Dubai Marina, a famous beachfront location in this energetic metropolis, offers its inhabitants a variety of opulent living experiences that blend urban sophistication with waterfront tranquility.

What sets Dubai Marina apart is its meticulously planned infrastructure, world-class dining and entertainment options, and the unique experience of living alongside one of the world’s largest man-made marinas. The community offers everything from sleek studio apartments to expansive penthouses with panoramic views.

For buyers seeking a combination of lifestyle, convenience, and investment potential, Dubai Marina apartments represent one of the most compelling options in the city’s dynamic property market.
author: BlackOak Advisory

title: Dubai has not experienced a slowdown in economic growth
excerpt: There are several key factors that drive Dubai's remarkable growth, and its potential to surpass records in real estate transactions.
content:
There are several key factors that drive Dubai’s remarkable growth, and its potential to surpass records in real estate transactions continues to attract global attention.

Dubai’s economy has proven resilient through multiple global cycles, supported by diversification strategies, a pro-business regulatory environment, and strategic investments in infrastructure, tourism, and technology. The real estate sector has been a primary beneficiary of this economic strength.

Record-breaking transaction volumes, rising property values, and an influx of international investors all point to a market that shows no signs of slowing down. Dubai’s forward-looking policies and ambitious development plans ensure sustained momentum for years to come.
author: BlackOak Research

title: Palm Jumeirah Villa for Sale – Perfect for your Family
excerpt: A unique artificial island in the form of a palm tree, complete with the trunk, crescent, and 17 fronds, is a famous site showcasing architectural brilliance.
content:
A unique artificial island in the form of a palm tree, complete with the trunk, crescent, and 17 fronds, is a famous site showcasing architectural brilliance and luxury living at its finest.

Palm Jumeirah villas offer families an unparalleled lifestyle — private beach access, stunning sea views, and a sense of community that is hard to find elsewhere in Dubai. The villas range from contemporary designs to Mediterranean-inspired estates, each offering generous living spaces and premium finishes.

For families seeking the ultimate in luxury, privacy, and convenience, a Palm Jumeirah villa represents the pinnacle of Dubai living.
author: BlackOak Advisory

title: Why Dubai Hills Is The Perfect Place to Live I Buy Villas in Dubai Hills
excerpt: This brand-new community-style living area, loved by residents and investors, offers the best in luxury suburban living.
content:
This brand-new community-style living area, loved by residents and investors, offers the best in luxury suburban living. Dubai Hills Estate has rapidly become one of the most sought-after communities in the city.

With its championship golf course, extensive parks and green spaces, premium retail at Dubai Hills Mall, and proximity to key business districts, Dubai Hills Estate delivers a lifestyle that appeals to families, professionals, and investors alike.

The community’s master plan ensures a balanced mix of villas, townhouses, and apartments, creating a diverse and vibrant neighbourhood that continues to appreciate in value.
author: BlackOak Communications

title: Dubai Luxury Property Market Sets New Records in 2025
excerpt: Dubai's luxury real estate segment continues its unprecedented growth trajectory, with ultra-prime transactions exceeding AED 100 million becoming increasingly common.
content:
Dubai’s luxury real estate market has reached new heights in 2025, with the ultra-prime segment recording transaction volumes that surpass all previous records. Properties priced above AED 50 million have seen a 45% increase in sales compared to the previous year.

The surge in demand is driven by a combination of factors including Dubai’s favorable tax regime, world-class infrastructure, and the continued influx of high-net-worth individuals. Emirates Hills, Palm Jumeirah, and Dubai Hills Estate have emerged as the top-performing neighbourhoods.
author: BlackOak Research

title: Does Dubai need more malls? The people say yes
excerpt: In a city that already has more than 60 million square feet of leasable retail space, one of the highest per capita in the shopping-crazy Gulf, it is perhaps surprising to see more malls being built or added to the pipeline.
content:
In a city that already has more than 60 million square feet of leasable retail space, one of the highest per capita in the shopping-crazy Gulf, it is perhaps surprising to see more malls being built or added to the pipeline.

Yet that is exactly what is happening in Dubai, where this week Sobha Realty said it has started construction on a new 339,000sq ft mall at its Sobha Hartland community at a price of AED210 million ($57.22 million).

This follows the much glitzier announcement by Emaar in June that it would spend 1.5 billion dirham ($408 million) to add 204 shops, restaurants and cafes to the already massive 12 million sq ft Dubai Mall – which in 2023 became the most visited place in the world with 105 million customers, according to Emirati news agency Wam.

Experts say these two classes of retail centres, the smaller community mall and the "headline grabbing" mega-mall, exemplify precisely what’s driving Dubai’s success and growth in this industry.

“Dubai’s world-class mega-malls are very much part of Dubai’s tourism offering and are extremely popular with both tourists and residents all year round,” says Andrew Elliott, director of commercial agency at Chestertons Mena.

“Community malls offer on-the-doorstep shopping, dining and entertainment for families and individuals,” he says.

“With new residential developments on the way and existing developments continuing to expand, there is still plenty of opportunity for local community malls.”

Also slated for delivery this year are another 1.8 million sq ft of leasable area, according to research company Nikoliers, including across expected malls such as the Al Khail Avenue mall and the Dubai Expo mall, though just how much progress is there on those is unclear.

The new Sobha Realty mall will be relatively contained in size, with about 35 stores, restaurants, gyms and other amenities.

It is designed to cater to the needs of growing residential communities east of Dubai Design District, which currently have little retail access outside of Dubai Mall, more than 10km of busy highways away.

Providing residents with a range of local conveniences that community malls typically offer – from grocery stores, pharmacies and medical clinics to small shops and restaurants – also fits the Dubai authorities’ newly announced embrace of the “20-minute city”.

“Take Dubai Hills Mall, for instance. The venue, which is quite large and high-end, has become popular among residents along Umm Suqeim Street, such as those from communities like Dubai Hills, Arabian Ranches and Moudon,” says Imran Sheikh, a partner at BlackOak Real Estate in Dubai.

“The retail space is filling up fast, and the mall is always packed. Contrary to expectations, it seems like there has been no cannibalisation of shoppers from Dubai Mall or Mall of the Emirates,” he says.

“So, Sobha building a new mall doesn’t surprise me. Malls closer to communities with a large radial reach will succeed.”

Haider Tuaima, director of real estate research for ValuStrat, has been in Dubai for almost 30 years and remembers when the city proudly counted seven malls in the mid-1990s.

It’s been a long journey to the 135 in operation today, but Tuaima believes there is definitely space for more due to population growth and the hot climate.

Tuaima says that a mall’s location, size and ability to target the right customer base will obviously have an impact on its success, with individual centres’ performances varying across the city and time.

But occupancy is high across the board, Tuaima says. ValuStrat data shows top-rated malls by Emaar Properties and Majid Al Futtaim securing 97 and 96 percent occupancy in 2023, respectively.

According to CBRE, average retail rents in Dubai rose 10.5 percent the first three months of this year over the same period in 2023 amid the “supply-and-demand imbalance”.

And then there’s the climate.

“For Dubai, and the Gulf in general, being under air conditioning for shopping, dining, if you are a family with kids and there is some sort of a place for them to play, is really crucial, because you’re looking at four to six months of hot weather,” says Tuaima.
author: BlackOak Research
